International Trade

Our Mission

MENU

Educational Objectives of the International Trade Program:

To      educate specialists who possess comprehensive theoretical and practical      knowledge of international trade; who are committed to ethical principles,      sustainability, and social responsibility; who are innovative and      responsive to technological advancements; who embrace lifelong learning;      who can communicate effectively across cultures; and who are able to use a      foreign language at a professional level.

To      educate professionals who can analyze the operational mechanisms and      strategies of international trade in order to serve in national and      international enterprises or public institutions; and who are capable of      making effective decisions by utilizing digital trade tools and      technological infrastructures, data analysis methods, and entrepreneurial      approaches.

To      educate individuals who possess theoretical and analytical knowledge in      the field of international trade; who have well-developed research skills;      who are proficient in digital and quantitative applications; who are      interested in project development and academic studies; and who are      prepared for graduate-level education.

To      educate individuals who can analyze international economic and commercial      relations; who possess strategic thinking, leadership, and teamwork      skills; who are sensitive to cultural diversity; and who demonstrate      strong communication abilities.
